Keep the manual toggle only for pure video/text lessons that have no submission/quiz. This removes a step from the two most-common completion paths without removing user control where it's the only signal.
findings.file: app/lib/scr-learn.ts (S.assignment submit handler, ~lines 349-359) + server /api/submissions completion side-effect

findings.problem: renderStudentSelf (scr-profile.ts:270-336) rebuilds the level/XP/streak panel ('Tu rango' card, lines 316-325) and the highlighted-badges card (lines 326-333) — both are smaller copies of what progress and badges already show, and once SIMP-01 lands, of what lifetimeProfile will show. The student profile thus becomes a fourth place to see level+XP+streak+badges.
findings.rootCause: Profile was given progress widgets for completeness before the dedicated progress/lifetime screens existed.
findings.userImpact: More duplicated numbers across screens; the profile reads as a progress dashboard rather than an identity/bio page.
findings.recommendation: Trim the student profile right rail to identity + enrolled programs, and replace the duplicated 'Tu rango' and 'Insignias destacadas' cards with a single 'Ver mi trayectoria' link to lifetime. Keeps profile focused on who-you-are; progress numbers live in one place.

findings.problem: The single-column parentPortal stacks a lot for one screen: a 4-KPI strip, a pending-approvals card, per-child cards (each with skills + attendance + achievements + upcoming), then a right rail with Seguridad+consentimiento card, public-profile per-child toggles, a Mensajes card, a Membresía+per-child-threshold card (membershipCard), a monthly-report card, AND a 'Vincular otro estudiante' card. The right rail alone is six stacked cards. Two of them (public-profile consent in the security card, and approval-threshold in the membership card) are both privacy/consent controls split across different cards.
findings.rootCause: Each PRD §11 sub-requirement (consent, public profile, threshold, report, link, membership) became its own card and they accreted in the right rail.
findings.userImpact: A non-technical parent faces ~10 distinct blocks; the two consent-style controls being in separate cards makes 'where do I control what my kid shares / what gets auto-approved?' harder than it should be.
findings.recommendation: Merge the per-child public-profile toggles and the per-child approval-threshold selector into a single 'Permisos y consentimiento por hijo' card (one row per child with both controls), and collapse the static 3-bullet 'Seguridad' reassurance into a short footnote rather than a full card. Cuts the right rail from 6 cards to ~4 and co-locates the consent decisions.
findings.file: app/lib/scr-parent.ts (membershipCard thresholds lines 330-353 + security/public-profile block lines 428-450)

findings.problem: The membership pricing grid always renders a third 'Elite' tier card (scr-lifetime.ts:544-556) whose every CTA is disabled 'Próximamente' and whose price is 'Muy pronto'. It occupies a full third of the pricing row purely to advertise something nobody can buy, splitting visual attention away from the one upgrade that exists (Pro).
findings.rootCause: Elite was included in the tier model for future-proofing and rendered as a full equal-weight card.
findings.userImpact: On the conversion-critical screen, a third of the decision area is a dead, non-actionable card — weaker focus on the only real CTA (Pasar a Pro).
findings.recommendation: Replace the full Elite card with a single slim 'OTR Elite — próximamente' teaser strip below the Free/Pro pair (hide-until-purchasable). Let the pricing grid be a focused 2-up Free vs Pro comparison so the Pro CTA dominates.

findings.problem: S.lesson carries a 10-line hardcoded 'defaultProse' Claim/Warrant/Impact essay (scr-learn.ts:551-560 and again referenced in scr-core.ts:551-571) rendered only when no lesson is selected (legacy entry without window.__lesson). In a real multi-course app the lesson screen is always reached with a selected lesson, so this fallback ships demo content that can only appear via an unreachable/legacy path — and it duplicates the same prose block in two files (scr-core.ts and scr-learn.ts both define S.lesson with the prose).
findings.rootCause: Early prototype rendered a fixed sample lesson; the demo prose was kept as a fallback when the data-driven lesson flow was added, and the lesson builder exists in two screen files.
findings.userImpact: Maintenance noise and a small risk of demo text leaking to a user who hits the screen without a lesson id; two divergent copies of S.lesson invite drift.
findings.recommendation: Replace the defaultProse fallback with the honest empty state already used elsewhere ('Selecciona una lección de tu curso' → go('course')), and delete the duplicate S.lesson definition so a single builder owns the screen. Removes ~12 lines of dead demo content and one duplicate.
findings.file: app/lib/scr-learn.ts (S.lesson defaultProse, lines 551-571) — reconcile with duplicate in app/lib/scr-core.ts S.lesson (lines 541-616)

findings.problem: S.teacher.render() concatenates two complete pages on one route: a tracking dashboard (page-head 'Seguimiento del grupo' + 4 KPIs + 7-column roster + 'Requieren atención' rail + 'Pendientes de calificar' card) and then managePanel() which is itself a full page (its own page-head 'Estructura del curso' + 4 more KPIs + the Cursos→Módulos→Lecciones→Examen tree). The coach lands on ~8 KPI tiles and two distinct primary objectives (monitor students vs. author content) with no single obvious next action. This is the densest screen in the app and the only one with two page-heads.
findings.rootCause: Two PRD jobs (group tracking and content management) were merged into one render to avoid a route, instead of being split into tabs or separate routes. managePanel is invoked inline at the end of render().
findings.userImpact: First-time and time-pressed coaches must scan ~8 numbers and two headers to orient; the genuinely urgent items (at-risk students, ungraded submissions) compete with course-structure KPIs that are reference data, not actions. Heavy vertical scroll, unclear where to start.
findings.recommendation: Split into two tabs on the teacher route ('Grupo' | 'Contenido') reusing the existing .tabs pattern (as debate/coachwork already do), OR move managePanel() to its own 'manage'/'course-builder' route (which already exists — there is a 'Editor completo' button to it) and replace the inline panel with a single compact link card. Either way render() should surface ONE primary action (Calificar entregas when pendingSubs>0, else first at-risk student). Drop the 4 'Estructura del curso' KPIs (Cursos/Módulos/Lecciones/Exámenes) from this screen — they are vanity counts, not decisions.

findings.problem: renderGrid() shows up to 11 filtering controls above the results: a row of up to 8 specialty chips (specs sliced to 8) plus three selects (Idioma / Precio / Orden). The user faces a wall of filter choices before seeing any coach, and specialties + sort + price overlap conceptually.
findings.rootCause: Every available filter dimension is exposed eagerly at the top of the grid rather than progressively (e.g., sort hidden behind a single control, specialties capped lower or collapsed).
findings.userImpact: A parent/student just trying to find a coach must parse 11 controls first; choice paralysis and slower time-to-first-result. Most users would pick a coach from the default 'Mejor valorados' sort without touching filters.
findings.recommendation: Reduce default visible controls: keep the specialty chips but cap to ~5 with a '+N más' expander; merge Idioma+Precio into a single 'Filtros' control or keep only the two highest-value filters (specialty + price) and demote Orden to a compact right-aligned sort dropdown. Results should be visible without scrolling past the filter bar. Files: scr-marketplace.ts renderGrid() lines ~236-273 (specs = ...slice(0,8); the three <select> blocks).

findings.problem: Each roster row renders three actions — 'Adjudicar', 'Evaluar', and an icon-only message button — repeated for every student. The repeated 3-way decision per row multiplies choices (Hick), and the message control is a 30x30px icon-only button (icon-btn width:30px;height:30px, scr-teacher.ts:615) with only title= and no aria-label, below the 44px touch/Fitts target and weak for screen readers.
findings.rootCause: All row-level actions are surfaced inline at equal weight; the icon-only button relies on title (not announced reliably, not keyboard-discoverable) and is undersized.
findings.userImpact: On a large roster the coach re-evaluates three buttons per student; on touch/mobile the 30px message target is hard to hit; screen-reader users hear no accessible name for the message action.
findings.recommendation: Demote to one primary row action plus an overflow: keep 'Adjudicar' (or 'Evaluar') visible and move the rest into a kebab/'⋯' menu, OR drop the per-row message icon (messaging is reachable from the student detail). For any icon-only control add aria-label and bump to 36-44px min target. Same fix applies to the icon-only message button in S.teacher 'Requieren atención' rows (scr-teacher.ts:117) and the 30x30 message button at scr-teacher.ts:615.

findings.lens: Nielsen — match between system and real world + content clarity
findings.problem: The third KPI tile is labeled 'Engagement promedio' but is fed by k.onTime (the 'puntualidad'/on-time field). The code comment at scr-teacher.ts:79 explicitly notes the value derives from Enrollment.engagement, yet the column header in the roster below is 'Engagement' too — so two different concepts (a KPI labeled engagement-but-sourced-from-onTime, and a per-row engagement pill) sit feet apart with overlapping names.
findings.rootCause: A label was changed to 'Engagement' without renaming the underlying field, leaving a label/source mismatch acknowledged only in a comment.
findings.userImpact: A coach can't tell whether 'Engagement promedio' means punctuality, attendance-adjacent activity, or the same scale as the roster's Alto/Medio/Bajo pills — eroding trust in the numbers that are supposed to drive intervention.
findings.recommendation: Make label and source agree: either rename the field/derivation so the KPI truly aggregates the same engagement scale shown in the roster pills, or rename the KPI back to what onTime measures (e.g., 'Puntualidad' / 'Entregas a tiempo'). Add a one-line tooltip stating how it's computed, consistent with the per-row pill semantics.
findings.file: app/lib/scr-teacher.ts (KPI tile line ~80, C.kpi('Engagement promedio', String(k.onTime)))

findings.problem: The hub exposes 6 sub-tabs (Resumen, Mis debates, Práctica, Leaderboard, Torneos, Analytics). Six is within Miller's 7±2 but at the top of it, and the navy hero already shows rating + recent form + 'Registrar un debate', which the 'Resumen' tab largely re-presents — so the first thing the user sees partly duplicates the first tab.
findings.rootCause: Tab set grew to cover every PRD §6 surface; hero and Overview were designed independently and overlap.
findings.userImpact: Mild — a competent user navigates fine, but the tab row plus a content-rich hero is a lot to absorb on entry, and the hero/Overview overlap is redundant scanning.
findings.recommendation: Leave the 6 tabs (each is a real destination) but de-duplicate: since the hero already carries rating/form/record-CTA, trim the Overview tab to the NON-hero content (e.g., next steps + quick analytics teaser) rather than restating rating/form. No tab removal needed; this is content reduction, not redesign.
findings.file: app/lib/scr-debate.ts (TABS array lines 27-34; heroPanel ~113-150; Overview section after line 160)

confirmedHigh.problem: Course/coach names containing '&' render the literal entity '&' on the catalog cards (and elsewhere). queries.ts builds DB.catalog with already-escaped values (queries.ts:1430 `name: esc(pickLang(...))`, `coach: esc(...)`, `format: esc(...)`, `modality: esc(...)`), but S.catalog re-escapes them.
confirmedHigh.rootCause: Double-escape: S.catalog interpolates esc(c.name)/esc(c.coach) over values that queries.ts already passed through esc(). Same class as the leaderboard double-escape fixed in a062cd5.
confirmedHigh.userImpact: On primary discovery surface a course literally reads 'Oratoria &amp; Speaking' (already & in capture, doubles again here), undermining the premium feel and trust. Affects every name with '&'.
confirmedHigh.recommendation: In app/lib/scr-extra.ts S.catalog.card, drop the redundant esc(): use ${c.name}, ${c.coach}, ${c.code} (code is not esc'd in queries so keep esc(c.code)). Verify against queries.ts:1430 which fields are pre-escaped and only esc the raw ones. Do the same audit, not blanket-strip.

verdict.reason: Substantiated in source. queries.ts:1469-1470 pre-escapes catalog fields (`name: esc(pickLang(c.name,c.nameEn))`, `coach: esc(c.coachName)`, `format: esc(...)`, `modality: esc(...)`), while `code: c.code` stays raw. scr-extra.ts S.catalog.render (card lines 185-189) re-applies esc(): `${esc(c.code)}` (correct, code is raw), but `${esc(c.name)}` (188) and `${esc(c.coach)}` (189) double-escape already-escaped values. esc() (esc.ts:10) maps & -> &, so a name with '&' becomes '&amp;'. The output is injected via root.innerHTML (Aula.tsx:68) with no decode step, so it renders the literal text '&'. Real seed data triggers it: prisma/seed.ts:347 course name 'Oratoria & Speaking'. This is the IDENTICAL class to the leaderboard double-escape fixed in a062cd5 (scr-debate render crudo, queries is sole escaper). The recommendation is correct and surgical: drop esc() on c.name/c.coach, keep esc(c.code). Only inaccuracy is the cited line number (finding says queries.ts:1430; actual is 1469) — field-level claim is exact. Cannot refute.

confirmedHigh.problem: The 'Personas' section of search is permanently empty for the student role — the only role that can reach search. S.search reads DB.students (scr-extra.ts:297), but queries.ts only populates base.students inside the isTeacher block (queries.ts:1514-1518); for a STUDENT it is undefined, so (DB.students||[]) yields [] every time. Result count and the people group are silently always courses-only.
confirmedHigh.rootCause: Data contract mismatch: search consumes a teacher-only collection. Students hit search via the topbar input (Aula.tsx:953-955), but never have DB.students; teachers/admins have it but use dedicated roster screens instead.
confirmedHigh.userImpact: A student searching a classmate's name gets 'Sin resultados' even when the person exists. The advertised people-search is dead for its primary audience — a broken feature presented as working.
confirmedHigh.recommendation: Either (a) expose a role-safe, privacy-scoped people index to students in queries.ts (e.g. cohort/leaderboard-derived rows with name+level only, same opt-in rules as leaderboard) and point S.search at it; or (b) if people-search is out of scope for students per PRD, remove the 'Personas' section from S.search and reword the page-sub to 'cursos' so the count and copy are honest. Prefer (b) if no privacy-cleared people source exists.

verdict.reason: I attempted to refute this but the source confirms it. (1) S.search reads `(DB.students || [])` at scr-extra.ts:297 and renders the "Personas" group from it (line 307). (2) `base.students` is assigned ONLY inside the `if (isTeacher)` block (queries.ts:1522-1526), where `isTeacher = me?.role === "TEACHER" || me?.role === "ADMIN"` (queries.ts:192). grep confirms no other assignment of DB.students for students. So for a STUDENT, DB.students is undefined and the filter always yields []. (3) Reachability holds: the topbar searchbox input is rendered for ALL roles in shell.ts:173-176 (only the "create" button at line 180 is teacher-gated), and Enter on it calls renderApp("search") (Aula.tsx:953-955). Students therefore reach search but never have a people index. (4) It is NOT already handled — there is no role guard, no [A11Y]/audit marker on S.search. Decisively, scr-core.ts:312 carries an explicit audit note documenting that the dashboard previously had this exact "DB.students is teacher-only → never renders for the student" bug and was fixed by switching to the canonical per-user DB.leaderboard.rows; S.search was simply never given the same fix. That precedent corroborates the diagnosis and validates recommendation (a) — DB.leaderboard is in the shared base object (queries.ts:1495), available to all roles with a session. Cited files/lines are accurate. I am downgrading severity only: this is a silently-omitted sub-section (the section() helper returns "" when count is 0, so no broken empty box appears) on a secondary screen; course search still works and counts/copy stay self-consistent. A genuine dead-feature for its primary audience, but not a crash/data-integrity/security issue, so "medium" is the honest level rather than "high".

confirmedHigh.problem: The quiz answer options (.q-opt) and question-jump dots (.q-dot) are plain <div>s with click listeners attached in mount(); they have no role, no tabindex, and are never focusable. A keyboard or screen-reader user literally cannot select an answer or navigate questions — the single most important interaction in the Learn flow is mouse-only. The global [A11Y-01] Enter/Space handler in Aula.tsx (line 960) only fires for elements matching [role=button][tabindex], which these never get (unlike the .mitem and <tr> rows in scr-core.ts which DO get role/tabindex).
confirmedHigh.rootCause: q-opt/q-dot were built as styled divs with imperative click wiring in S.quiz.mount() and were never given role/tabindex, so they fall outside the project's own [A11Y-01] keyboard-activation contract.
confirmedHigh.userImpact: Keyboard-only and assistive-tech students cannot take any quiz. There is no fallback. This blocks course completion (quizzes gate progress) for an entire access class.
confirmedHigh.recommendation: In S.quiz.mount() paint(), render each .q-opt with role="radio" tabindex="0" inside a role="radiogroup" (aria-labelledby the question), and each .q-dot with role="tab"/button tabindex="0"; add an aria-checked/aria-current state. Either reuse the [A11Y-01] handler (give them role=button tabindex=0) or add keydown(Enter/Space + Arrow keys) handling in mount(). Add :focus-visible is already global so focus ring comes for free once they are focusable.
confirmedHigh.file: app/lib/scr-learn.ts → S.quiz.mount() paint() (.q-opt / .q-dot rendering, ~lines 448-453); contract in app/components/Aula.tsx line 960

verdict.reason: I attempted to refute this but the source fully substantiates it. In app/lib/scr-learn.ts S.quiz.mount() paint() (lines 448-453), each answer is rendered as `<div class="q-opt ..." data-o=...>` and each jump as `<span class="q-dot ..." data-d=...>` — plain elements with NO role and NO tabindex. Selection/navigation is wired only via imperative `el.addEventListener('click', ...)` at lines 451 and 453. grep of the whole file confirms zero occurrences of tabindex, role, or any keydown/focus handling in scr-learn.ts. The global [A11Y-01] handler in app/components/Aula.tsx is exactly at line 960 and only activates `[role="button"][tabindex]` on Enter/Space — which these divs never match. The contrast cited is real: scr-core.ts gives its clickable containers `role="button" tabindex="0"` at lines 176, 390, and 527, so the quiz divs fall outside the project's own keyboard-activation contract. The global `:focus-visible` rule (app.css line 19) exists but is moot since the elements can never receive focus. This is a genuine WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard, Level A) failure on the single core interaction of the quiz screen, with no keyboard/AT fallback to select answers or submit. Because quiz completion gates course progress (quizResults courseProgress/xpGain), keyboard-only and screen-reader students are fully blocked. File/line references, root cause, and recommendation are all accurate. Severity critical is justified — it blocks an entire access class from completing assessments.

confirmedHigh.lens: Content reduction / Nielsen (match real world) / Honesty
confirmedHigh.problem: The 'Calificación' card hardcodes an identical rubric for EVERY 100-point assignment — Estructura (CWI) 30 / Claridad y voz 25 / Evidencia 25 / Tiempo y cierre 20 (lines 196-199). This is presented as if it were this specific activity's grading breakdown, but it is a static debate-speech template shown regardless of what the assignment actually is (e.g. a written essay or a research upload). For non-100-point assignments it correctly falls back to a generic message — proving the 100-point branch is fabricated specificity.
confirmedHigh.rootCause: A demo placeholder rubric was left inline in the render template instead of reading a real rubric from L (e.g. L.rubric).
confirmedHigh.userImpact: Students optimize toward criteria that may not apply; coaches grade on different criteria than shown. Erodes trust the moment a student notices two unrelated assignments share the same '30/25/25/20' rubric.
confirmedHigh.recommendation: Render the rubric only from real data (L.rubric / L.rubricItems). If absent, use the same honest generic message already used for non-100-point assignments ('Tu coach la revisará y te dará una nota con feedback'). Remove the hardcoded array at lines 197-198.

verdict.reason: The hardcoded array exists exactly as cited (scr-learn.ts:196-199), gated on `maxPoints === 100`, with an honest generic fallback in the else branch — file, symbol, and line numbers are all accurate. The Lesson/assignment data model genuinely has NO rubric field (prisma schema lines 196-218: only type, submitKinds, maxPoints, contentHtml); the one `rubric String?` in the schema (line 710) belongs to CoachSession (1:1 coaching), not assignments. So the rubric is static, not data-driven — the smell is real. HOWEVER the 'high' severity and its user-impact narrative are refuted by the data: NO seeded assignment in course-templates.ts is 100 points (actual values: 20/25/30/40/50, every single one). Therefore the hardcoded branch is unreachable by all current assignment content — every real assignment renders the honest generic fallback. The branch only fires via the null→100 UI default (line 90) for a coach-created assignment with points left blank, which does not occur in the captured demo data. The claimed scenario ('student notices two unrelated assignments share 30/25/25/20', 'optimize toward criteria that may not apply') cannot occur in the product's actual state. This is a latent/dormant placeholder tied to a magic number, a low-severity content cleanup — not a visible honesty/trust violation. Recommendation (read from L.rubric / fall back to the generic message) is reasonable but L.rubric does not exist in the schema, so the fix is simply removing the dead 100-point branch.

confirmedHigh.problem: The debate history cards (viewHistory) and tournament cards (viewTournaments) are clickable <div class="tile click"> elements carrying data-debate / data-tournament-card, but they have no role="button", no tabindex, and no aria. The screen's mount() wires a plain click listener (root.querySelectorAll('[data-debate]')), so a mouse opens the ballot modal but a keyboard user cannot focus or activate the card at all. The app's own [A11Y-01] handler in Aula.tsx (line 960) only fires .click() for elements matching '[role="button"][tabindex]' — these cards match neither, so they are completely keyboard-inoperable.
confirmedHigh.rootCause: Clickable behavior was attached via data-attribute + JS listener without the semantic/keyboard scaffolding the rest of the app standardized on (role=button + tabindex=0), so the central A11Y-01 keydown bridge never picks them up.
confirmedHigh.userImpact: Keyboard-only and screen-reader users cannot open a debate ballot or inspect a tournament — a primary interaction of the flagship screen is unreachable without a mouse. Fails WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
confirmedHigh.recommendation: In scr-debate.ts add role="button" tabindex="0" (and an aria-label like 'Ver ballot vs <opponent>') to the history tile (line 225, data-debate) and the tournament card (line 355). The existing Aula.tsx onKey bridge will then activate them on Enter/Space — or add an explicit keydown handler in mount() alongside the click listener. Tournament cards that are purely informational (no data-tournament action) should NOT get role=button; only the ones that open detail.
confirmedHigh.file: app/lib/scr-debate.ts (viewHistory line 225 tile; viewTournaments line 355 tile) + verify against Aula.tsx onKey line 960

verdict.reason: Partially substantiated but materially overstated; downgraded to medium. CONFIRMED: scr-debate.ts line 225 renders the debate-history ballot opener as `<div class="tile click fade-up" data-debate=...>` with no role/tabindex/aria, and mount() (lines 490-492) wires only a plain `click` listener with no keydown companion. The recommended fix (add role=button tabindex=0 so the existing onKey bridge activates it) is correct. REFUTED / INACCURATE on the second cited location: line 355 `data-tournament-card` is NOT a broken interactive element — there is no `[data-tournament-card]` click listener anywhere in mount(); it is a dead/decorative container. The defect is one control type on a secondary sub-tab (history); the card's headline data (result/opponent/format/delta) is plain readable text, only the deep ballot modal is gated; and the identical `tile click`+data-attr+modal pattern with the same gap also exists in scr-marketplace.ts line 189 (data-coach) — so this is a contained, systemic minor a11y pattern gap, not a flagship-specific blocker. confirmedHigh.problem: Interactive/important green text uses `--action` (#2CAA20), which is 3.05:1 on white and 2.83:1 on cream — below the 4.5:1 AA threshold for normal text. The design system already ships `--otr-green-text` (#176B11 = 6.67:1) created expressly for green text, so this is avoidable drift.
confirmedHigh.rootCause: .sky, .eyebrow and .cc-pct were defined against the saturated brand green (--action / --action-hover) rather than the AA-safe text token --otr-green-text; the marketplace inherits those classes.
confirmedHigh.userImpact: Low-vision users and anyone on a bright screen struggle to read the only inline CTA ("Ver perfil"), the section label, and prices — the prices are the single most important data point for a marketplace decision.
confirmedHigh.recommendation: Repoint the text-only green to the AA token: change `.sky{color:var(--action)}` (app.css:24) to `color:var(--otr-green-text)`, and `.course-card .cc-pct{...color:var(--action-hover)}` (screens.css:42) / the reused .cc-pct figures to `--otr-green-text`. The eyebrow (.eyebrow, app.css:118) should also move to --otr-green-text. Pure-green stays fine for fills/icons; only text must change.

verdict.reason: Substantiated in source. Cited symbols/lines are accurate: app.css:24 `.sky{color:var(--action)}`, app.css:118 `.eyebrow{...color:var(--action)}`, screens.css:42 `.course-card .cc-pct{...color:var(--action-hover)}`. The marketplace uses all three: class="sky" "Ver perfil" link (scr-marketplace.ts:213, 12.5px/600), class="eyebrow" (line 243, 11px/700), and class="cc-pct" prices (lines 211/385/405 at 14-15px/800). Computed WCAG ratios confirm the failure: --action #2CAA20 = 3.05:1 white / 2.83:1 cream (well below 4.5:1 AA for normal text, which the eyebrow and the small bold link both are); --action-hover #1E8C16 = 4.36:1 white / 4.04:1 cream (marginally below 4.5:1). The finding's "≈3.9:1" for prices is slightly understated (real 4.04-4.36) but the conclusion (fails 4.5:1) holds. The AA-safe token --otr-green-text #176B11 (6.19-6.67:1) genuinely exists with an explicit "WCAG AA >=4.5:1" comment (tokens.css:17) and is already used by sibling components (.btn-soft app.css:138, .badge.sky app.css:198), so the "avoidable drift" framing is correct. No [A11Y-*] marker or per-element override mitigates these. Two reasons I correct high->medium: (1) the price figures fail only marginally via --action-hover, and the large 22px/800 figure (line 453) actually passes the 3:1 large-text threshold; (2) the "Ver perfil" link is a redundant affordance since the whole coach card is clickable, softening the user impact the finding emphasizes. The worst, clear-cut failures are the eyebrow label and the inline CTA text at 2.83:1, which is a genuine AA defect worth fixing.
