globals.summary: The top-level IA is genuinely strong: the nav was deliberately reorganized to the PRD §3.1 map (Principal / Aprender / Centro de progreso / Marketplace / role workspaces / Ajustes), role-scoped sidebars + tabbars exist for all four roles, breadcrumbs are navigable, and the explore↔marketplace duplication is an intentional, documented alias (not a defect). Findings cluster around three real IA gaps rather than structural rot: (1) no client-side role-gate on student-only routes — coach/parent/admin can reach Niveles/Trayectoria/Cursos/Debate via the always-present topbar search or stray go() calls, where they get student-shaped, often inert results; (2) the global topbar search is mounted for every role but only ever returns student courses + a student roster, making it noise-to-harmful for coach/parent/admin; (3) a concrete mislink in the coach onboarding empty-state that sends a coach to the read-only PUBLIC coach view (wrong nav highlight + "OTR/Coach" orphan crumbs) instead of their editable profile. Lower-severity items: the coach "Coaches" item sits alone under "Principal" with no real home anchor, a dead hardcoded badge:'Varsity' in source, and unknown-route clicks failing silently with no feedback.

The OTR Aula design system is mature and unusually disciplined for an in-progress product: tokens.css is the single source of truth, raw hex in the 21 scr-*.ts builders is near-zero (9 literals total, mostly intentional), the brand color semantics hold (black = primary CTA, green = action/accent, gold = achievement-only — no gold misuse found anywhere), and modal accessibility is centralized via a MutationObserver in Aula.tsx that retrofits aria-modal/labelledby/focus-trap/Escape onto every dynamically-inserted .modal (so the role=dialog-only inline markup is intentional and correct — NOT a finding). The real debt is component drift in three small but repeated controls and one inconsistent class contract: (1) the affirmative-green button .btn-accent is fully defined in CSS but used 0 times — dead code that invites future inconsistency; (2) the on/off switch is hand-rolled inline 3 times with a real color drift (settings uses --otr-green, lifetime uses --otr-sky-lo for the identical "on" state); (3) the language toggle is copy-pasted between shell.ts and scr-settings.ts; (4) btn-soft (54x) and btn-ghost (48x) are used interchangeably for the same secondary-action role with no rule, including "Volver" rendered as both; (5) the systemic & double-escape (known bug) is rooted in an inconsistent escaping contract — queries.ts escapes at the data layer, but some builders re-esc() the same fields and some don't, with comments warning about it. Findings are ranked by user-visible impact; most are low-severity internal consistency.

The codebase has a genuinely above-average a11y baseline: the [A11Y-*] markers that exist hold up (toast live-region, centralized modal focus-trap/Escape/labelledby via MutationObserver, the global Enter/Space activator for role=button+tabindex elements, settings/leaderboard/privacy toggles correctly use <button role="switch" aria-checked>, form fields get for/id + aria-describedby + aria-invalid, and the focus ring token #176B11 is visible on cream). Token contrast is mostly compliant — text-2/text-3 on white and the black btn-primary (21:1) are excellent, and btn-accent (the only failing button style) is unused. BUT the [A11Y-01] keyboard activator silently misses several PRIMARY clickable surfaces because they are bare clickable <div>s that lack role="button"+tabindex: marketplace coach cards (data-coach), debate hub cards (data-debate), the course module accordion header (data-acc), and the trajectory skill rows (data-lpskill). These open detail views / expand content on mouse click but cannot be reached or fired by keyboard at all — the single highest-severity gap. Secondary: heading semantics are broken systemically — the page H1 is rendered as <div class="page-title"> in 42 places (only 2 real <h1>), and debate/marketplace/teacher/parent/admin/lifetime screens have NO <h1> at all, jumping straight to h3/h4 (fails 1.3.1/2.4.6 and breaks SR landmark navigation). Lower-severity: a few badge/eyebrow/tab green-on-tint combos land at 3.0–4.0:1 (fail AA normal text), and the rich-text editor's <label for> points at a contenteditable div it can't natively associate with.

OTR Aula is a polished, content-rich SPA, and most of its complexity is earned — but the student "Centro de progreso" is badly over-fragmented and several surfaces re-render the same data. The single highest-leverage simplification: the student nav exposes FOUR progress destinations (Mi trayectoria / Niveles / Mis calificaciones / Logros) plus the dashboard, and the skill radar, XP-to-next-level bar, and badge collection are each painted on 3+ separate screens (dashboard, profile, progress, lifetime, badges). `lifetimeProfile` is essentially a superset of `progress` and `badges`. Consolidating the Progress Center from 4 nav items to 2 (Trayectoria + Calificaciones) removes a whole tier of "which-screen-has-my-radar?" navigation cost with near-zero data loss. Secondary wins: the Events screen has no unique action (its tournaments just bounce to Debate Hub and its events duplicate the dashboard rail); the dashboard Achievements card repeats the hero CTA verbatim; the coach has three overlapping course-structure surfaces (teacher.managePanel + manage + course-builder); and several screens carry trimmable filler/duplicate-CTA noise. Findings are ranked by disappear-test impact. The two known bugs (& double-escape, .lb-wave hydration) are excluded per brief.

globals.area: Cognitive Load (Hick / Miller / Fitts / Jakob / Tesler)
globals.summary: The product is visually mature and consistent, so cognitive-load defects are concentrated in the supply-side (coach) screens and the marketplace, not in the polished student flows. The single worst offender is the coach "Seguimiento del grupo" screen (scr-teacher.ts): one route stacks TWO full pages — a tracking dashboard (page-head + 4 KPIs + 7-column roster + at-risk rail + pending-grading card) followed by an entire content-management panel with its own page-head + 4 more KPIs + course tree. That is ~8 KPIs and two competing primary objectives on a single scroll (Miller + visual-hierarchy failure; the screen has no obvious single "next action"). Secondary offenders: the marketplace presents up to 11 filter controls (8 spec chips + 3 selects) before the user has seen a single result (Hick); the Participants roster repeats a 3-action decision (Adjudicar/Evaluar/message) on every row with a 30x30px icon-only target (Hick + Fitts + a11y); and a coach KPI is mislabeled "Engagement promedio" while reading the onTime/puntualidad field (the code comment itself flags the mismatch — Nielsen match-real-world). The student dashboard's earlier "no next-lesson CTA" flow note is already resolved (lesson screen has Anterior/Siguiente). Most findings are merge/simplify/automate, not redesign.
